display.purpose: Hero and top-of-page h1s; flat, condensed uppercase Archivo at weight 800.

title.purpose: Component-level headings and button labels, e.g. option card titles.

body.purpose: All prose copy, generally at reduced ink opacity rather than a separate color token.

label.purpose: Micro-labels above form fields, nav sub-labels, section eyebrows.

shadows.name: panel
shadows.purpose: Flat by rule -- no elevation anywhere on the site.

shadows.name: panel-lg
shadows.purpose: Flat by rule -- no elevation anywhere on the site.

motion.name: reduced-motion-override
motion.purpose: Global prefers-reduced-motion collapse, applied to all elements.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Filled Signal Red CTA, used for the header's persistent 'Get a Quote' and in-task progress actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #EC3013; color: #F3F2F2; font-family: Archivo, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 0.875rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.03em; padding: 12px 24px; border-radius: 0; border: none; transition: background-color 0.2s ease-out, transform 0.2s ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #AE1800; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #EC3013; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Outlined marketing CTA, fills solid on hover. Default for every in-page 'Get a Quote' outside the header.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: #EC3013; font-family: Archivo, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 0.875rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.03em; padding: 12px 24px; border-radius: 0; border: 1px solid #EC3013; transition: background-color 0.2s ease-out, color 0.2s ease-out; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: #EC3013; color: #F3F2F2; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #EC3013; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Neutral outlined button for non-primary actions like 'Compare Options'.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: transparent; color: #201E1D; font-family: Archivo, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 0.875rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.03em; padding: 12px 24px; border-radius: 0; border: 1px solid rgba(32,30,29,0.2); transition: border-color 0.2s ease-out; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: rgba(32,30,29,0.5); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #EC3013; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard form field, e.g. quote form contact/vehicle fields, with error state.
components.html: We need a valid VIN.
components.css: .ds-label { display: block; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: #605D5D; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-input { width: 100%; background: #F3F2F2; color: #201E1D; border: 1px solid rgba(138,135,133,0.6); border-radius: 0; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: Archivo, sans-serif; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #8A8785; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #EC3013; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-input-error { border-color: #AE1800; } .ds-error-text { margin-top: 4px; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #AE1800; }

components.name: Transport Option Card
components.description: Signature decision card for Carrier Transport vs Personal Driver -- solid fills, not tints, per the One Accent Rule.
components.html:
Carrier Transport

Your vehicle rides on an insured, licensed carrier's hauler.
components.css: .ds-option-card { border-radius: 0; padding: 32px; color: #F3F2F2; } .ds-option-card--carrier { background: #EC3013; } .ds-option-card--personal-driver { background: #201E1D; } .ds-option-title { font-family: Archivo, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.25rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.03em; } .ds-option-copy { margin-top: 12px; font-family: Archivo, sans-serif; color: rgba(243,242,242,0.8); }

components.name: Route Rule Divider
components.description: Solid 2px section divider -- flat, unbroken line, not a dashed motif.

components.name: Manifest Label
components.description: Micro-label used above form fields, in nav sub-labels, and as the hero eyebrow.
components.css: .ds-manifest-label { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: #605D5D; }

narrative.overview: Royal Rollers now reads like a printed transport manifest run through a Modernist type foundry, not a stamped steel plate and not a SaaS product page. Every surface is flat: off-white paper, near-black ink, one red accent, nothing else. There is no gradient, no shadow, no rounded corner anywhere on the site. Structure comes entirely from solid 2px rules dividing sections the way a manifest divides line items, condensed uppercase Archivo doing the work a heavier display face used to do, and monospace reserved for anything that is literally data. This still explicitly rejects the generic SaaS look -- no gradient-heavy hero, no glassmorphism, no soft-shadow cards, nothing that reads as a lead-gen funnel dressed up as a product -- and adds the Modernist system's own constraint on top: no second hue. The former two-accent system (a teal for Carrier Transport, an oxblood for Personal Driver) is gone. The two service types are now told apart by the only two tones the system has: red fill for Carrier Transport, black fill for Personal Driver. A third, functional-only color (brass-dark) exists solely to make error and danger states visible; it is never used as decoration.
keyCharacteristics: Flat off-white paper background, near-black ink text, one saturated red accent -- no second brand hue anywhere
Archivo carries both display and body type; JetBrains Mono is reserved for VINs, ZIPs, dollar amounts, and other structured data
Zero border radius on every interactive surface -- buttons, inputs, cards, badges, step indicators
Zero drop shadows -- separation comes from solid 2px rules and flat color blocks, never elevation
Carrier Transport = solid red; Personal Driver = solid black -- the one semantic color pairing the whole site relies on
brass-dark, a deeper shade of the same accent, is reserved exclusively for error/danger states so they stay visually distinct from both normal text and the Carrier Transport brand color

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Signal Red is the only saturated color on the site. There is no secondary brand hue. Carrier Transport is solid Signal Red, Personal Driver is solid Ink. highway and rust are kept as token names in code purely so components don't need renaming -- highway resolves to Signal Red, rust resolves to Ink.

rules.name: The Functional-Red Rule
rules.body: brass-dark (#AE1800) exists only to make error, danger, and failure states visible. It is never used decoratively. It is deliberately the same hue family as Signal Red but dark enough to stay distinct from both the bright Carrier Transport red and plain ink text.

rules.name: The No-Warmth Rule
rules.body: No cream, beige, or brass-toned neutral anywhere. Paper is a true cool off-white, not a warm parchment tone.

rules.name: The Data-Reads-As-Data Rule
rules.body: Any field the user or system treats as structured data -- VIN, ZIP, dollar amounts -- renders in JetBrains Mono, even inline. Never let a data value inherit body prose styling.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Archivo is the only sans-serif on the page, in both display and body roles. Don't introduce a second display face; weight and size carry the hierarchy.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Rule Rule
rules.body: Elevation tokens exist in code purely so components don't need editing, but they are permanently set to none. Never reintroduce a real box-shadow value, and never add an ad hoc shadow to a component.

dos: Do render structured data (VIN, ZIP, dollar amounts) in JetBrains Mono, uppercase where applicable, everywhere it appears.
Do use the solid 2px rule (rgba(32,30,29,0.4)) for every section divider -- never a dashed line, never a soft gray hairline.
Do keep every interactive element's corner radius at zero -- buttons, inputs, cards, and badges all share this value.
Do keep Carrier Transport = Signal Red and Personal Driver = Ink as a fixed, exclusive pairing.
Do use brass-dark for every error, danger, or failure state so they stay visually distinct from plain Ink text and the Signal Red brand color.
Do carry the global :focus-visible Signal Red outline and prefers-reduced-motion collapse into any new component.
donts: Don't use a gradient-heavy hero, glassmorphism, or a generic-tech-startup visual language.
Don't add a drop shadow anywhere -- shadow-panel/shadow-panel-lg resolve to none at the token level.
Don't use a warm cream/beige background or a warm brass/gold accent.
Don't let a data value inherit body prose styling -- it must render in the mono Label face.
Don't soften corners on new components -- zero radius is the system-wide rule.
Don't use plain Ink for an error or danger state -- it's indistinguishable from normal text. Use brass-dark.
Don't introduce a second saturated hue for a new 'option' or category -- use the Signal-Red/Ink pairing instead.
